judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports are very similar and equally thorough on (a), (b), and (d); Report 2 is stronger on (c), explicitly endorsing that doc updates should land in the same commit/PR as the code going forward, which Report 1 only implies.

report_1.evidence: The sanctioned integration point is `medibill/backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... A refund function (e.g., `refund(...)`) should be added to `gateway.py`... `medibill/backend/app/api/invoices.py:4,14`... `medibill/backend/app/workers/dunning.py:2,9`... `app/billing/errors.py` (which only re-exports `chargekit.errors` classes...) as the sole documented exception.

report_2.evidence: New refund calls belong in `backend/app/billing/gateway.py`... The only sanctioned exception is `medibill/backend/app/billing/errors.py:6`, which imports just `chargekit.errors` for exception types and explicitly documents itself as the boundary exception. `invoices.py` and `dunning.py` are not on that exception list and are undocumented violations...

report_1.evidence: `InvoiceOut` in `backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22` has a sixth field, `external_ref`... This field was added by migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_2.evidence: The actual response, `InvoiceOut` in `medibill/backend/app/models/invoice.py:16-22`, has six fields — the five listed plus `external_ref`. That field was added in commit `c23da88`... via migration `0006_add_invoice_external_ref.sql`

report_1.evidence: `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` should be updated now, as a fix-up (or at minimum flagged in the refund PR), rather than deferred to "later"... worth fixing before it happens a third time with refunds.

report_2.evidence: the fix is now overdue technical debt and should be corrected before or alongside starting the refund feature, not bundled invisibly into the refund PR... the same commit/PR that adds `gateway.refund()` must update the doc again if it adds any new webhook types... and that update must be called out explicitly in the PR description per project documentation's guidance.

report_1.evidence: The actual handler, `medibill/backend/app/api/webhooks.py:10-19`, handles **5** types... I would change the doc to list all 5 types explicitly... or better, replace the hardcoded count with a reference to the code as the source of truth

report_2.evidence: I would change the wording to drop the count and either enumerate the current list accurately... or, better, describe the property instead of listing/counting
